assets.abstract: The cutaneous senses are traditionally thought to comprise four recognized submodalities that relay tactile, thermal, painful and pruritic (itch) information to the central nervous system, but there is growing evidence for the presence of a fifth modality that conveys positive affective (pleasant) properties of touch. Cutaneous sensory channels can be further classified as serving predominantly either discriminative or affective functions. The former provides information about the spatial and te

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Social Incarnation — intersubjectivity, embodied empathy, social predictive processing, second-person neuroscience.
